title: Damage
excerpt: Damage is the loss inflicted on a creature or object when an attack, hazard, spell, or other harmful effect successfully takes effect.
markdown:
# Damage

Damage is the loss inflicted on a creature or object when an attack, hazard, spell, or other harmful effect successfully takes effect.

## Rules Use

Damage reduces a target's staying power. For creatures, that usually means reducing current [[hit_points]]. For objects, it means degrading whatever durability the relevant rule tracks.

## Applying Damage

- damage is applied after the effect that caused it successfully resolves
- the amount can come from a fixed value, a [[damage_roll]], or another rule-defined source
- the target's current state can change the final result through mitigation, vulnerability, or other modifiers

## Common Outcomes

Damage can wear down a target over time, overwhelm temporary buffers such as [[temporary_hp|Temporary Hit Points]], or push a creature toward defeat, unconsciousness, or death.
